# UC-18.2.7 · VMware NSX Micro-Segmentation Policy Drift Detection — Intent-vs-Realized DFW Revision Tracking, Approved Baseline Comparison, Out-of-Band Edits, Section Order Reconciliation

## Description

Micro-segmentation posture is only as trustworthy as the **Distributed Firewall** policy graph you believe is in force. When **`Policy API`** intent drifts away from an **approved baseline** — because someone edits **`security-policies`** through the UI without a ticket, automation applies a partial merge, GitOps falls behind live NSX, or an adversary alters intent before realization — the organization inherits a silent gap between what compliance reports say the network enforces versus what **`vsipfwwd`** actually programs on transport nodes. Regulators and internal audits increasingly expect provable alignment between change records, identity of mutating principals, section ordering (**`Ethernet`**, **`Emergency`**, **`Infrastructure`**, **`Environment`**, **`Application`**, **`Local Gateway`** categories in **NSX 4.x**), and **`revision_id`** monotonicity per domain and **`policy_id`**. Drift therefore becomes a governance and cyber-resilience signal, not merely a firewall engineering detail: stale baselines masquerade as incidents, emergency break-glass events need retrospective attestation, and multi-tenant **`domain_name`** namespaces (`default`, **`cgw`**, **`mgw`**, tenant slices) amplify blast radius whenever a shared Manager cluster serves mixed lifecycles.

## Value

Splunk operationalizes continuous **configuration compliance** by joining high-signal **`vmware:nsxt:policy_revision`** (or syslog policy events) with **`nsx_policy_baseline.csv`**, enriching with **`vmware:nsxt:audit`** for **`client_ip`**/**`user_name`** accountability, and elevating **`realization_status`** mismatches (**`ERROR`**, lingering **`UNREALIZED`**) ahead of dataplane surprises. Teams gain **`out-of-band`** visibility: changes without ServiceNow **`CHG`** correlation surface in the same pane as **`terraform_ansible_pipeline_revision`** noise that you can suppress using automation netblocks. Dashboards expose **intent-vs-realized** risk, section **reordering** (**`SECTION_ORDER_CHANGED`**), import failures, and **`domain_name=test_*`** versus production baselines so test-driven drift does not page production owners. Pairing results with **`UC-18.2.1`** hit counters shows when policy edits changed meaning even while rule-hit volumes look calm.

## Implementation

Install **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** where **`vmware:nsxt:audit`** and **`vmware:nsxt:syslog`** parse; schedule HTTPS collectors or **`nsx_policy_collector`** scripted inputs that poll **`GET /policy/api/v1/infra/domains/<domain>/security-policies/<policy_id>`** and emit **`vmware:nsxt:policy_revision`** with stable **`revision_id`** fields; maintain **`transforms.conf`** lookups for **`nsx_policy_baseline.csv`** keyed on **`domain_name`** + **`policy_id`**; optionally replicate ServiceNow **`CHG`** rows for correlation macros; save **`uc_18_2_7_nsx_policy_drift`** with throttle keys **`domain_name`**, **`policy_id`**.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• **Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt** (**Splunkbase 4856**) must parse **`vmware:nsxt:audit`**, **`vmware:nsxt:syslog`**, and optional **`vmware:nsxt:realization`** on Search Heads plus Heavy Forwarders hosting modular HTTPS collectors authenticated through **`POST /api/session/create`** cookies or mutual TLS toward each NSX Manager VIP / cluster hostname.
• Collector identities require `infra` plus `policy` scopes covering `GET /policy/api/v1/infra/domains/<domain>/security-policies/<policy_id>` and sibling `rules/<rule_id>` routes — avoid recycling interactive administrator passwords; vault PEM bundles inside Splunk credential stores with ninety-day rotations aligned to appliance certificate lifecycle guidance.
• Reserve `index=vmware` / `index=vmware_nsx` retention adequate for **`revision_id`** diff replay (≥ thirteen months when SOC2 evidence packs demand quarterly snapshots).
• Maintain **`nsx_policy_baseline.csv`** with authoritative **`approved_revision_id`** derived from Terraform `terraform state pull` hashes, GitOps `HEAD` merges, or CAB-signed YAML exports — columns **`domain_name`**, **`policy_id`**, **`approved_revision_id`**, `last_approval_change_id`, `owner_team`, `last_approval_timestamp` stay mandatory for **`lookup`** joins.
• Pair **`vmware:nsxt:audit`** **`request_uri`** trails with **`change_user`** columns whenever **`FIELDALIAS`** maps `user_name` ↔ **`Authentication.user`** so **`out-of-band`** hunts retain **`client_ip`** evidence separate from syslog-derived **`POLICY_UPDATE`** tokens alone.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Install **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** from Splunkbase 4856 on parsing tiers first — validate **`props.conf`** extracts `revision_id`, `policy_id`, **`domain_name`**, **`total_rules`**, **`realization_status`** before layering scripted collectors.
(2) Stand up **`nsx_policy_collector`** (Python modular input or Universal Forwarder `script://`) polling each watched `domain` (`default`, `cgw`, `mgw`, tenant namespace) via HTTPS every two to five minutes, persisting normalized JSON lines as **`vmware:nsxt:policy_revision`**, including top-level **`sequence_number`** plus child rule counts mirrored in **`total_rules`**.
(3) Forward NSX Manager `/var/log/syslog` (RFC 5424 priority preserved) into **`vmware:nsxt:syslog`** so spontaneous **`SECTION_ORDER_CHANGED`**, **`IMPORT_FAILED`**, and **`REALIZATION_FAILED`** anecdotes appear even between collector polls — size **Splunk Connect for Syslog** pools when **`POLICY_DELETE`** bursts arrive during migrations.
(4) Publish **`transforms.conf`** `[nsx_policy_baseline]` pointing at **`lookups/nsx_policy_baseline.csv`** with `match_type = WILDCARD(domain_name)` only when deliberately sharing one row across multiple test domains; default exact match on `domain_name` + `policy_id` prevents accidental bleed between tenants.
(5) Automate baseline refresh using a CI webhook firing a saved search that **`outputlookup`** refreshes `nsx_policy_baseline.csv` after each merged Terraform apply — stamp `last_approval_timestamp` using `strftime(now(),"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")` so **`cmdb_baseline_lag_window`** reviewers understand freshness gaps.
(6) Mirror optional `vmware:nsxt:audit` enrichment fields (**`client_ip`**, **`request_uri`**, `outcome`) alongside `splunk_audit` correlation IDs whenever Splunk OTEL collectors coexist — aids **`terraform_ansible_pipeline_revision`** suppression lists keyed by automation subnets.

Step 2 — SPL search and alert wiring
Embed the primary `spl` field inside a macro **`macro_nsx_policy_drift_base`** once fieldaliases stabilize — keeps `revision_id` parity identical across dashboards versus paging alerts.

```spl
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx OR index=network_ops)
(sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:policy_revision" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:syslog")
earliest=-24h@m
| eval domain_name=trim(coalesce(domain_name,domain,domain_short,""))
| eval policy_id=trim(coalesce(policy_id,policy_uuid,security_policy_id,""))
| eval revision_id=trim(coalesce(revision_id,policy_revision_id,policy_rev,""))
| eval change_user=trim(coalesce(change_user,changed_by,actor,user_name,""))
| eval change_ts_raw=coalesce(change_timestamp,policy_change_ts,_time)
| eval change_timestamp=strftime(change_ts_raw,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval total_rules=tonumber(coalesce(total_rules,rule_total,count_rules,"0"))
| eval realization_status=upper(trim(coalesce(realization_status,policy_realization_status,intent_status,"")))
| eval syslog_evt=case(
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)policy_update"),"POLICY_UPDATE",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)section_order_changed"),"SECTION_ORDER_CHANGED",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)rule_added"),"RULE_ADDED",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)rule_deleted"),"RULE_DELETED",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)rule_modified"),"RULE_MODIFIED",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)realization_failed"),"REALIZATION_FAILED",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)import_failed"),"IMPORT_FAILED",
    1=1,"")
| eval feed_kind=if(sourcetype=="vmware:nsxt:policy_revision","policy_feed",if(len(syslog_evt)>0,"syslog_evt","other"))
| lookup nsx_policy_baseline.csv domain_name policy_id OUTPUT approved_revision_id owner_team last_approval_change_id last_approval_timestamp
| eval approved_revision_id=trim(coalesce(approved_revision_id,""))
| eval drift_core=if(len(approved_revision_id)>0 AND revision_id!=approved_revision_id,1,0)
| eval realization_problem=if(realization_status=="ERROR" OR syslog_evt=="REALIZATION_FAILED",1,0)
| eval section_order_flag=if(syslog_evt=="SECTION_ORDER_CHANGED",1,0)
| eval import_problem=if(syslog_evt=="IMPORT_FAILED",1,0)
| where drift_core=1 OR realization_problem=1 OR section_order_flag=1 OR import_problem=1
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen latest(revision_id) as revision_id latest(approved_revision_id) as approved_revision_id latest(change_user) as change_user latest(change_timestamp) as change_timestamp latest(total_rules) as total_rules latest(realization_status) as realization_status latest(syslog_evt) as syslog_evt latest(feed_kind) as feed_kind values(owner_team) as owner_team latest(last_approval_change_id) as last_approval_change_id by domain_name policy_id
| eval change_timestamp=strftime(last_seen,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z")
| sort - last_seen
| head 400
```

Augment downstream joins:

`| join type=left **change_user** [| search index=vmware sourcetype="**vmware:nsxt:audit**" earliest=-24h | **stats** latest(**client_ip**) as **client_ip** latest(**request_uri**) as **request_uri** by user_name | rename user_name as **change_user** ]`

Schedule **`cron */15 * * * *`, throttle **`domain_name`** + **`policy_id`** for four hours, route `realization_problem=1` pages to Policy platform crews while `drift_core=1` routes through `owner_team` mappings embedded inside **`nsx_policy_baseline.csv`**.

Step 3 — Validate against authoritative consoles
(a) Inside NSX Manager navigate Security ▸ Distributed Firewall ▸ Policies, filter **`domain_name`**, expand **`policy_id`** — compare **`revision_id`** labeled in the UI JSON view (**Policy API** browser) versus Splunk **`revision_id`** fields.
(b) Cross-check **`terraform plan`** when GitOps manages the same **`security-policies`** object — terraform drift should match Splunk `drift_core` when **`approved_revision_id`** references the commit actually applied; divergence here points to `state file` staleness rather than NSX lying.
(c) Validate ServiceNow **`CHG`** numbers for each alert window — absence flags **`out-of-band`** status; presence should still be compared to `change_user` because service accounts occasionally land outside expected SCG groups.
(d) Proof-read **`policy_**realization_status**`** via Monitor ▸ Realization (NSX 4.x) ensuring Splunk **`realization_status`** strings map one-to-one — when UI shows `REALIZED` yet Splunk reads `ERROR`, investigate `Central Control Plane` backlog rather than indexer latency first.
(e) Run `GET /policy/api/v1/infra/domains/<domain>/**security-policies**/<policy_id>/rules/<rule_id>` sampling `_revision_id` per rule when diagnosing **`rule_order_renumber`** versus semantic edits — Splunk stores aggregates only; granular pulls remain authoritative.

Step 4 — Operationalize dashboards and paging hygiene
• Publish Dashboard Studio workbook **`ds_nsx_policy_drift.json`** featuring row-one `baseline freshness` gauges plotting `now()-last_approval_timestamp` per `domain_name`, row-two drift heat-map (`domain_name` × `policy_id`), row-three `top` panel summarizing `change_user` counts filtered `feed_kind=syslog_evt`, row-four `timeline` overlay for **`SECTION_ORDER_CHANGED`** markers stacked above **`REALIZATION_FAILED`** bursts.
• Pipe `realization_status=ERROR` storms into incident bridges referencing **`bulk_policy_import_migration`** markers — **`IMPORT_FAILED`** spikes concurrent with **`terraform apply`** outages merit **`CHG`** bridges distinct from attacker hypotheses.
• Embed **`suppress_windows`** **lookup**s keyed by **`terraform_ansible_pipeline_revision`** Jenkins hashes when **`vmware:nsxt:audit`** `client_ip` aligns with **`runner_net`** CIDR lists approved quarterly by Platform Security.
• Mirror **`policy_feed`** staleness KPI when scripted collectors stall — absence of **`vmware:nsxt:policy_revision`** rows longer than twice poll interval triggers infra ticket separate from `drift_core` positives.

Step 5 — Tuning and extensions
• Correlate with **`UC-18.2.1`** `vmware:nsxt:firewall` `RULE_TAG` trending — semantic drift sometimes hides inside steady hit counters until `services` bindings mutate silently.
• Layer **`SECTION_ORDER_CHANGED`** detections against **`sequence_number`** CSV snapshots exported nightly — suppress **`rule_order_renumber`** noise when hashed `rule_signature` stays invariant via Splunk `sha256` macro on sampled rule bodies.
• Integrate `CHG` replication from ServiceNow `incident` or `change_request` indexes using `correlation_id` extracted from `vmware:nsxt:audit` `request_uri` query parameters when operators embed ticket numbers in descriptions.
• Extend multi-tenant domains by maintaining separate **`nsx_policy_baseline.csv`** files per business unit (`bu_a`, `bu_b`) merged through **`lookup`** helper macros that prepend namespace to `policy_id` when conflicts arise.
• Tie back to Terraform `state` drift detectors — when **`terraform plan`** reports no diff yet Splunk `revision_id` climbs, investigate NSX GUI sessions `outcome=success` without matching **`terraform apply`** — often manual hotfix.
• Escalate sustained **`REALIZATION_FAILED`** clusters to VMware GSS with Support Bundles referencing Splunk timelines — indicate whether **`vsipfwwd`** dataplane mismatches coincide with `transport_node` upgrades (**`**UC-18.2.1**7`**) versus pure control-plane faults.
• Maintain runbook appendix listing **`emergency_section_override`** workflow: page Security Engineering first, annotate retroactive `last_approval_change_id`, do not silently delete Splunk alerts.

Troubleshooting quick hits

• `lookup` misses — CSV row keys missing trailing domain suffix cause null **`approved_revision_id`** → false negatives; normalize lowercase `domain_name` before comparison.
• **`stats`** duplication — overlapping modular inputs double-emit identical `revision_id`; **dedup**e via `**dedup** domain_name policy_id` upstream when `source`/`host` duplicates surface after TA upgrades.
• **`strftime`** blanks — malformed `change_timestamp` from REST lacking timezone offsets — coerce `strptime` with explicit `%z` once HTTPS collectors stamp RFC3339 literals.
• ` syslog_evt` empty — syslog forwarded without keywords due to `facility`** filtering — widen syslog routing map before blaming Splunk SPL logic.


## SPL

```spl
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx OR index=network_ops)
(sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:policy_revision" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:syslog")
earliest=-24h@m
| eval domain_name=trim(coalesce(domain_name,domain,domain_short,""))
| eval policy_id=trim(coalesce(policy_id,policy_uuid,security_policy_id,""))
| eval revision_id=trim(coalesce(revision_id,policy_revision_id,policy_rev,""))
| eval change_user=trim(coalesce(change_user,changed_by,actor,user_name,""))
| eval change_ts_raw=coalesce(change_timestamp,policy_change_ts,_time)
| eval change_timestamp=strftime(change_ts_raw,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval total_rules=tonumber(coalesce(total_rules,rule_total,count_rules,"0"))
| eval realization_status=upper(trim(coalesce(realization_status,policy_realization_status,intent_status,"")))
| eval syslog_evt=case(
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)policy_update"),"POLICY_UPDATE",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)section_order_changed"),"SECTION_ORDER_CHANGED",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)rule_added"),"RULE_ADDED",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)rule_deleted"),"RULE_DELETED",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)rule_modified"),"RULE_MODIFIED",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)realization_failed"),"REALIZATION_FAILED",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)import_failed"),"IMPORT_FAILED",
    1=1,"")
| eval feed_kind=if(sourcetype=="vmware:nsxt:policy_revision","policy_feed",if(len(syslog_evt)>0,"syslog_evt","other"))
| lookup nsx_policy_baseline.csv domain_name policy_id OUTPUT approved_revision_id owner_team last_approval_change_id last_approval_timestamp
| eval approved_revision_id=trim(coalesce(approved_revision_id,""))
| eval drift_core=if(len(approved_revision_id)>0 AND revision_id!=approved_revision_id,1,0)
| eval realization_problem=if(realization_status="ERROR" OR syslog_evt="REALIZATION_FAILED",1,0)
| eval section_order_flag=if(syslog_evt="SECTION_ORDER_CHANGED",1,0)
| eval import_problem=if(syslog_evt="IMPORT_FAILED",1,0)
| where drift_core=1 OR realization_problem=1 OR section_order_flag=1 OR import_problem=1
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen,
       latest(revision_id) as revision_id,
       latest(approved_revision_id) as approved_revision_id,
       latest(change_user) as change_user,
       latest(change_timestamp) as change_timestamp,
       latest(total_rules) as total_rules,
       latest(realization_status) as realization_status,
       latest(syslog_evt) as syslog_evt,
       latest(feed_kind) as feed_kind,
       values(owner_team) as owner_team,
       latest(last_approval_change_id) as last_approval_change_id
  by domain_name policy_id
| eval change_timestamp=strftime(last_seen,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z")
| eval snap_token=upper(domain_name."#".policy_id)
| eval drift_family=case(realization_status=="ERROR","realize_fault", syslog_evt=="SECTION_ORDER_CHANGED","section_move", syslog_evt=="IMPORT_FAILED","import_fault", syslog_evt=="REALIZATION_FAILED","realize_syslog", revision_id!=approved_revision_id,"baseline_gap", 1=1,"signal_other")
| eval correlation_digest=len(revision_id)+len(approved_revision_id)+len(policy_id)
| fillnull value="none" syslog_evt feed_kind
| sort - last_seen
| head 400
| table last_seen domain_name policy_id revision_id approved_revision_id change_user change_timestamp total_rules realization_status syslog_evt feed_kind owner_team last_approval_change_id
```

## Visualization

(1) Heat-map **`domain_name`** × **`policy_id`** coloring **`drift_core`**. (2) Timeline **`revision_id`** escalations versus **`approved_revision_id`**. (3) Column chart top principals from **`change_user`** where **`feed_kind=syslog_evt`**. (4) Single-value gauges for **`realization_status=ERROR`** density. (5) Baseline freshness strip comparing **`last_seen`** to **`last_approval_timestamp`** from **`nsx_policy_baseline.csv`**. (6) Table pairing **`SECTION_ORDER_CHANGED`** flags with category **`sequence_number`** context exported beside **`policy_feed`** snapshots.
